behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: YES — an in-process mock won't validate that the request is correctly serialized for the real HTTP API (e.g., taxonomy could be malformed JSON, in wrong field, URL-encoded incorrectly). REAL-INFRA: YES — the request format depends on the actual HTTP contract with the external API. Only a fake HTTP server running locally can validate request structure without hitting the real API.
behaviors.test_description: Create a fake HTTP server that acts as the classification API. Configure it to validate that every request includes all taxonomy labels in the expected format. Call the module, make a request, then verify the fake server received the complete taxonomy list with correct serialization. Fail the test if any request lacks taxonomy or has malformed structure.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns 'unknown' instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: NO — we can easily mock the API to return an invalid label and verify the module's response. MOCK-HIDE: NO — mocking allows us to inject the exact error condition. This is pure input validation / error handling logic, no infrastructure dependency. The failure mode (forwarding invalid label) is caught by a deterministic mock returning an out-of-taxonomy value.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test with mocked API. Configure mock to return label 'unknown-category' (not in taxonomy). Call module.classify(). Assert the return value is 'unknown', not 'unknown-category'. Also test that valid labels are forwarded unchanged.

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: YES — a deterministic mock API always returns the same value, hiding whether the module actually caches. LLM-DEP: YES — the requirement inherently depends on handling real LLM nondeterminism (real LLMs can produce different outputs for identical inputs). MOCK-HIDE: YES — naive mocking would always pass since mocks are deterministic by default. To catch the real failure (module lacks caching when LLM is nondeterministic), need a real LLM that can produce different outputs to see if the module handles it.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with a real classification API (or staging environment). Call module.classify() twice with the same document. Due to LLM nondeterminism, the API might return different labels on each call. Verify the module returns the same label both times (cached) rather than calling the API twice. Inspect request logs to confirm only one API call was made.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test must call a real LLM to observe nondeterminism; mocking would hide the failure.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: YES — a mock API doesn't consume real tokens; it can't reveal inefficiency or overage. LLM-DEP: YES — actual token consumption depends entirely on what the real LLM generates in response. MOCK-HIDE: YES — a mock can always return a fake token count within budget, hiding the real failure that actual tokens exceed budget. Only a real LLM API call will show actual token usage.
behaviors.test_description: Call module.classify() with a typical document against a real API (production or staging). Capture the actual token usage reported by the API response. Verify that token_count <= configured_budget. Test with several representative documents at different lengths to confirm typical usage stays within limits.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: NO — we can mock the API to return HTTP 429 on the first call and success on the second, fully simulating the error condition. MOCK-HIDE: NO — mocking allows us to inject and verify the retry logic. REAL-INFRA: NO — rate-limit errors are just HTTP status codes; no real network or external service needed. This is pure retry/backoff logic, testable with in-process mocks.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test with mocked API. Configure mock to raise rate-limit exception on first call, succeed on second call. Verify that module.classify() retries and returns the successful result (not an error). Verify the module waited (check call timing or verify sleep was called with appropriate duration). Test edge case: multiple consecutive rate-limit responses before success.
